title: Tube Loss based Deep Networks For Improving the Probabilistic Forecasting of Wind Speed
authors: Not specified in the provided text
abstract: The paper introduces Tube Loss based Deep Networks for improving probabilistic forecasting of wind speed, addressing challenges related to volatility and uncertainty in wind energy systems.

introduction.background: Wind energy is a rapidly growing renewable energy source with unlimited availability and minimal environmental impact. In 2023, new global wind power capacity is expected to surpass 100 GW for the first time, with projections reaching 143 GW by the end of the decade.
introduction.problem: The high volatility and uncertainty of wind speed present significant challenges for maintaining safe and stable power system operation, scheduling turbine maintenance, and integrating with the power grid.
introduction.importance: Effective wind speed modeling and prediction plays a crucial role in the overall wind power generation process.

categories.name: Parametric or Distribution-based models
categories.description: Assume the predictive distribution and estimate parameters efficiently

examples.method: Mixture of Gaussian density with deep neural architectures

categories.name: Distribution-free methods
categories.description: Do not assume a specific distribution

Mixture of Gaussian density for modeling the predictive distribution by stacking different deep neural architectures
